intro: The most concrete split between Chargebee and Paddle is the pricing model: Chargebee runs on hybrid pricing from $599/mo (Performance), with a free tier, while Paddle uses usage-based pricing from 5% + $0.50/transaction. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Chargebee carries medium lock-in (medium — deep integrations, underandwithon logic) and hybrid pricing from $599/mo (Performance), with a free tier; Paddle carries high lock-in (high — mor model = paddle handles everything, fromwith adapts under paddle) and usage-based pricing from 5% + $0.50/transaction. Chargebee is a fit when you are saas. Paddle is a fit when you are saas, b2b. The honest trade-off: Chargebee is explicitly not for simple projects; Paddle is explicitly not for marketplace/platform payments. On the product side, Chargebee is described as Subscription and revenue management platform supporting SaaS, e-commerce, and box subscriptions with Stripe and Braintree, and Paddle is described as Merchant of Record and subscription billing platform for B2B/B2C SaaS with global tax, checkout, and revenue recovery.
quick_take: Chargebee is for saas; Paddle is for saas, b2b; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Chargebee if you need a free tier to start before committing budget, and your use case matches saas.
verdict_b: Choose Paddle if its usage pricing model fits your budget shape better than Chargebee's hybrid model, and you are saas, b2b.

faq.a: Chargebee starts at $599/mo (Performance); Paddle starts at 5% + $0.50/transaction. On entry price alone that is the comparison, but real cost scales with users, events or volume, which the entry prices do not capture. Check each vendor's full tier list before committing.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Chargebee to Paddle?
faq.a: Migration from Chargebee to Paddle is feasible but not trivial: Chargebee has medium lock-in (medium — deep integrations, underandwithon logic). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

Both tools tie at 5/5 for developer experience on our scoring. The real difference will show up in your specific workflow, not the headline rating. A one-hour spike on both is the fastest way to decide.

faq.q: Is Paddle a good alternative to Chargebee?
faq.a: Paddle is pitched at saas, b2b, which overlaps with Chargebee's fit (saas) but is not identical. Note that Paddle is explicitly not for marketplace/platform payments, so it only replaces Chargebee if you are outside that exclusion. The lock-in profile differs (medium vs high), which is the main thing to weigh.
